description: Showing careful consideration for others; thinking deeply before acting. 'Phenui' names the quality of considered attention—the mind that pauses to think of how actions affect others.
sound_symbolism: The word considers carefully. 'Phe' /ɸe/ begins with gentle breath, consideration forming. Then 'nu' turns inward, thought deepening. Finally 'i' arrives at understanding, consideration complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'phenui miona' (thoughtful person). Related to 'thesa' (careful) but emphasizes consideration of others rather than attention to detail.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Thoughtful communication considers impact before speaking—words chosen with awareness of how they will be received.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Right speech requires thoughtfulness—considering whether words are true, kind, and timely before uttering them.
